zh-hans.<Sullington, Kent,, Eng.>: 英国英格兰素可素林頓

nameMeanings.name: Wallis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: from Anglo-Norman French Waleis, Walais ‘Welshman’, also sometimes ‘Breton’ (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning a ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). In western and central England the medieval reference is clearly to Welshmen. In Norfolk and Lincolnshire, the reference was probably to Bretons, many of whom settled in the eastern counties after the Conquest. Compare Welsh , Walsh . The idea that the Scottish surname refers to the Welsh-speaking Britons of Strathclyde is erroneous.

variants.name: Wallace
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Wallis  , especially in Scotland, where the name was introduced from the Welsh Marches by a family of tenants of the Stewarts in the 12th century.
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, e.g.   Wallach  .

variants.name: Wales
meanings: habitational name from Wales near Sheffield (South Yorkshire). The placename means ‘The Welshmen’ and denoted a settlement of Brittonic speakers living on the boundary between the Anglo-Saxon kingdoms of Northumbria and Mercia (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). See also Wallis .
habitational name from Wales, the country named from Old English wēalas ‘foreigners’ (especially those speaking Celtic or Romance languages). This meaning of the surname is quite rare.

variants.name: Welsh
meanings: Scottish, English, and Irish: ethnic name from Middle English welshe (Old English wēalisc, wēlisc) ‘foreign, Welsh’, for someone from Wales or a speaker of the Welsh language. Compare Walsh and Wallace .
Americanized form of Ukrainian and Jewish (from Ukraine) Voloshin or its Polish variant Wołoszyn (see Woloszyn ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard Wallis was born about 1230, in Kent, England, United Kingdom. He married Mrs Richard Wallis in 1262, in Sullington, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Wallis est né environ 1230, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Mrs Richard Wallis en 1262, à Sullington, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Wallis wurde um 1230 in Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat 1262 in Sullington, Sussex, England, United Kingdom Mrs Richard Wallis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Wallis è nato il circa 1230 nel Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui sposato Mrs Richard Wallis nel 1262, nel Sullington, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Wallis(은)는 대략 1230년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 태어났다. 그는 1262년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 설링턴에서 Mrs Richard Wallis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Wallis родился около 1230 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Richard Wallis в 1262 г. в местности суллингтон, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.
